rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena Request For Sanctions Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Declaration Of Vatche Zetjian
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by the Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. By will, decedent Vartkes Anivian left 20% of the reside of his estate to "the Los Angeles Chapter of the Central Committee of the Armenian Revolutionary Federation of the Western United States." There is a dispute as to whether the rightful beneficiary is ARF Dashnaksutyun, Western USA and Armenian Cultural Foundation ("ARF"), on the one hand, or Armenian Revolutionary Federation WUSA, Inc. ("FEDERATION"), on the other. ARF issued a Deposition Subpoena for Personal Appearance and Production of Documents and Things on FEDERATION. ARF agreed to continue the deposition multiple times at the request of FEDERATION. Ultimately, however, FEDERATION refused to appear on the mutually agreed upon date and failed to produce a single document, instead serving only unverified boiler plate objections as to the deposition demand and every single document demand. Now before the Court is ARF's Motion to Compel FEDERATION's Compliance with Deposition Subpoena; Request for Sanctions. No opposition to the motion has been filed. ARF's Motion to Compel FEDERATION's Compliance with Deposition Subpoena is GRANTED. "If, after service of a deposition notice, a party to the action or an officer, director, managing agent, or employee of a party, or a person designated by an organization that is a party under Section 2025.230, without having served a valid objection under Section 2025.410, fails to appear for examination, or to proceed with it, or to produce for inspection any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice, the party giving the notice may move for an order compelling the deponent's attendance and testimony, and the production for inspection of any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice." (CCP SEction 2025.450(a).) Here, the record shows that FEDERATION failed to serve valid objections. Generally, boilerplate objections that have the effect of obstructing discovery are improper and sanctionable. (See Korea Data Systems Co. Ltd. V. Sup. Ct. (1997) 51 Cal.App.4th 1513, 1516.) The answering party owes a duty to respond in good faith as best he or she can. (See Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 771, 783.) The record demonstrates that FEDERATION did not act in good faith and failed to file valid objections. ARF has set forth specific facts showing good cause justifying the production for inspection of documents electronically stored information, and tangible things described in the deposition notice. (See CCP Section 2025.450(b)(1).) ARF established by declaration that it has contacted the deponent to inquire about the nonappearance. (See CCP SEction 2025.450(b)(2).) If "good cause" is shown by the moving party, the burden is then on the responding party to justify its objections. (Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (Guess? Inc.) (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98.) FEDERATION has failed to even try to carry its burden to justify even a single objection. On this record, the court finds that FEDERATION's refusal to sit for deposition and refusal to produce documents and things are without substantial justification.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena Request For Sanctions Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Declaration Of Vatche Zetjian (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 2] FEDERATION shall produce responsive documents and things and appear for deposition within 15 days of service of notice of entry of this order. ARF's request for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. ARF is the prevailing party on this motion, and sanctions are mandatory. (See CCP Section 2025.450(g)(1).) Furthermore, the Court finds that FEDERATION has misused the discovery process by making unmeritorious objections to discovery without substantial justification in violation of CCP Sections 2023.010(d) and (e), 2025.450(g)(1) and 2025.480(j). FEDERATION'S actions are not substantially justified. Under the lodestar method the court finds the reasonable hourly rate to be $425 and the reasonable time spent on relevant tasks to be 11.2. FEDERATION shall pay to ARF $4,760.00 within 15 days of service of notice of entry of this order. ARF's request is denied to the extent it requests sanctions in excess of $4,760.00. ARF shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Calendar Matter: EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing on Ex parte Petition for Letters of Special Administration will be continued to Friday. March 11, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., to be heard concurrently with a competing Ex Parte Petition for Special Letters with General Powers.
